other_observers: Many, including Erika De La Rocha
description: I was walking along the Fountain Creek Regional Trail near south Rice's Pond when I saw an adult white ibis in the water just below the bank where the trail runs. It was primarily preening. Within ~10 minutes a jogger came by and caused the ibis to fly to the north end of the pond- out of sight in the reeds/ cattails. I was the finder of the bird as no one had previously reported the ibis in this very heavily visited hotspot. I sent out a message to the What's App 4 county group and received multiple replies from birders who wanted to come see this bird and wanted more details of where it was located. When I returned to the area about 30 minutes later, there were multiple birders enjoying good looks at the white ibis.
photographer: Photographer: Erika De La Rocha, Erika De La Rocha
